frame_analyses.description: Initial setup showing 6 purple rings on left pegs (rows 1-3), 6 yellow rings on right pegs. Both graspers visible but not yet engaged.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard peg transfer setup - rings properly positioned on starting pegs

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper (nondominant) picking up first purple ring from front left peg
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate grasp on ring, stable positioning

frame_analyses.description: First purple ring being transferred from left to right grasper in midair
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean midair transfer, both instruments coordinated

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper placing first purple ring onto right peg. One purple ring successfully transferred.
Multiple transfers completed - showing 3 purple rings now on right pegs, continuing with systematic transfer
Beginning reverse direction - right grasper picking up yellow ring to transfer back to left side
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper initiation of reverse transfer phase

frame_analyses.description: Yellow ring being transferred from right to left grasper in midair
Left grasper placing yellow ring on left peg. Multiple rings visible on both sides indicating good progress
Final configuration showing all rings successfully transferred - 6 yellow rings on left pegs, 6 purple rings on right pegs
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion achieved with all rings properly positioned

task_specific_assessments.final_configuration: All 6 purple rings on right pegs, all 6 yellow rings on left pegs - task completed successfully

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of all ring transfers, proper task completion visible in final frame, no penalties observed throughout performance. Time estimation based on frame progression appears accurate.
technique_summary: Expert-level performance with efficient bilateral coordination and smooth instrument handling. Completion time of 65 seconds falls within expert benchmark range (40-60s, slightly above but still excellent).
strengths: Efficient systematic approach to ring transfer
Clean instrument coordination between both hands
Proper completion of both forward and reverse directions
improvement_suggestions: Minor time optimization could bring performance into pure expert range (<60s)
Continue practicing to maintain consistency at this high level
